skill.name: ecommerce
skill.description: Guidance for designing e-commerce product, cart, checkout, payment, order, and inventory systems.
skill.summary: Plan and review core e-commerce workflows and platform integrations.
skill.author: DaichiHoshina

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak crypto, external command, reconnaissance, entropy, and hardcoded URL patterns. Manual review found markdown documentation, fenced examples, REST route text, and one public API documentation link with no executable commands, cryptography, system probing, exfiltration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Weak crypto alerts are false positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts occur in descriptive JSON and markdown metadata. No hashing, encryption, or insecure cryptographic algorithm implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain metadata or user-facing guidance. There is no executable crypto code or API usage in either reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Markdown fences are not shell execution
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick alerts match markdown code fences used for examples. They do not execute commands or interpolate user-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks delimit markdown examples for state transitions, TypeScript, YAML routes, and output formats. No runtime shell, Ruby, or command invocation exists.

low_findings.title: Documentation URL and route examples are benign
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to public STORES retail API documentation. The reconnaissance alerts are REST endpoint examples, not host or system probes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed content contains only documentation references and example HTTP routes. There is no code that performs network access or reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Entropy alert is readable markdown content
low_findings.description: The high entropy alert appears to be caused by Japanese markdown text and front matter. The file is readable plain text with no encoded payload.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual inspection shows ordinary YAML front matter and Japanese description text. No binary, encrypted, compressed, or obfuscated content was found.

content.user_title: Build E-commerce Checkout and Store Systems
content.value_statement: E-commerce projects need reliable product, cart, order, payment, and inventory workflows. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured checklist for design, implementation, and review.
actual_capabilities: Defines core entities for products, variants, carts, orders, customers, and payments.
Outlines order and inventory state transitions for checkout workflows.
Provides requirement checklists for catalog, cart, payment, customer, legal, and security areas.
Shows an optimistic locking pattern for inventory reservation.
Lists REST-style endpoints for product, cart, and order APIs.
References Shopify, STORES, and custom platform integration topics.
limitations: It is guidance documentation, not a complete application framework.
It does not connect to live payment, store, or database services.
It does not replace legal, PCI DSS, or privacy compliance review.
Platform details must be verified against current vendor documentation.

use_cases.title: Plan a new online store
use_cases.description: Create the first domain model, checkout flow, and API outline before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Review checkout reliability
use_cases.description: Check cart, payment, inventory reservation, timeout, and rollback behavior against expected commerce workflows.

use_cases.title: Prepare platform integration work
use_cases.description: Compare Shopify, STORES, and custom build concerns before choosing integration boundaries.

prompt_templates.title: Create the store model
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new store
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design an e-commerce domain model with products, variants, carts, orders, customers, and payments. Include key fields and relationships.

prompt_templates.title: Design checkout states
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a checkout workflow for my store. Include cart confirmation, inventory reservation, payment success, payment failure, order confirmation, and timeout release.

prompt_templates.title: Review API coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my e-commerce API plan for product listing, product detail, cart item creation, cart item deletion, order creation, and order detail retrieval.

prompt_templates.title: Audit launch readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my e-commerce launch plan for security, PCI DSS scope, legal pages, inventory consistency, payment rollback, performance targets, and platform integration gaps.

output_examples.input: Design the checkout flow for a fashion store.
output: Cart confirmation reserves selected variants for a limited window.
Payment starts after shipping, coupon, and tax totals are final.
Payment failure releases inventory and keeps the cart recoverable.
Successful payment creates an order and moves it to fulfillment preparation.

output_examples.input: Review my product and inventory requirements.
output: Product CRUD and variant management are covered.
Real-time inventory updates need a concurrency control rule.
Search and filters are included, but low-stock alerts are not specified.
Legal pages and privacy requirements should be tracked before launch.

output_examples.input: Compare custom store work with Shopify integration.
output: Shopify reduces catalog, checkout, and admin build scope.
A custom build gives more control over inventory and checkout behavior.
Headless Shopify can separate storefront design from commerce operations.

best_practices: Reserve inventory with concurrency controls before starting payment.
Avoid storing card data directly and keep PCI DSS scope small.
Define timeout and rollback behavior before checkout implementation.
anti_patterns: Treating cart contents as guaranteed inventory without reservation.
Building payment success paths without failure and timeout recovery.
Launching without legal pages, privacy policy, and audit logging.

faq.question: Does this skill write a full e-commerce application?
faq.answer: No. It guides design and review of store workflows, domain models, API plans, and implementation requirements.

faq.question: Can it help with payment integrations?
faq.answer: Yes. It outlines checkout and payment flow concerns, but vendor setup must follow current provider documentation.

faq.question: Does it support Shopify work?
faq.answer: Yes. It references Shopify development topics, Storefront API, Admin GraphQL, and Hydrogen for headless commerce.

faq.question: Can it review an existing store design?
faq.answer: Yes. Use it to compare product, cart, order, payment, inventory, security, and performance coverage.

faq.question: Does it handle compliance automatically?
faq.answer: No. It lists PCI DSS, privacy, legal, and security checks, but expert compliance review is still required.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
